summary.intent: Apply a second-pass of Codex review findings to the carousel component, fixing 6 new issues around controlled-index sync, input normalization, autoplay edge cases, and pointer cancel handling.
summary.outcome: All 6 findings implemented and verified: controlled-index sync via on_props_changed/SyncControlledIndex event, slides_per_move/slides_per_view init normalization, PointerCancel re-arm, AutoPlayTick boundary guard, and is_playing fix. 105 tests pass, clippy clean, xci-fast green, committed and pushed.

repo: Controlled prop sync pattern: on_props_changed emits a SyncControlled* event when the controlled prop changes; the transition arm calls sync_controlled(Some(v)) to update the internal slot. Checkbox is the canonical example.
sync_controlled(Some(v)) is the mechanism in ars-core to set the controlled slot for a Bindable prop.
Edition 2024 with let-chains is in use across the workspace; clippy enforces collapsing nested if-let into a single let-chain.
xci-fast is the pre-push CI gate used in this workspace.
Spec files live under spec/components/layout/ and must be kept in sync with implementation changes; spec validation is part of the test suite.

code.finding: Added SyncControlledIndex event variant and on_props_changed override to emit it when the controlled index prop changes externally.
slides_per_move and slides_per_view are now normalized at init time (N2/N5 findings).
AutoPlayTick transition arm now has a boundary guard to prevent out-of-range advances.
PointerCancel now re-arms the drag state correctly (N3 finding).
is_playing computation fixed in both auto_play_trigger_attrs and auto_play_indicator_attrs (N4 finding).
Spec updated to reflect all 6 new findings: on_props_changed, SyncControlledIndex arm, init normalization, AutoPlayTick guard, PointerCancel re-arm, and is_playing fix.

workflow: Codex is used as an async AI code-review tool; results are polled via a background task and fetched as structured threads.
TDD tests are appended to the existing test module for each new finding before marking the task complete.
clippy is run as part of the build+test+clippy carousel gate, and nested if-let issues must be resolved before pushing.
The workflow is: implement fixes → build+test+clippy → write tests → sync spec → validate spec → xci-fast → commit+push.

friction: Clippy required collapsing a nested if-let into a let-chain in on_props_changed; required a follow-up edit after the initial build.
